title: Public opinion trajectory on indoor mask mandates in the United States during 2021

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Interpolated based on CDC guidance shifts in April 2021
Estimated: Reflects post-CDC May 13th guidance update allowing vaccinated people to go maskless
Estimated: Reflects Delta variant emergence and early local mandate re-impositions

events.description: CDC announces fully vaccinated people can resume activities without wearing masks
CDC updates guidance recommending masks for vaccinated people in areas of high transmission
President Biden announces federal vaccine and masking requirements for large employers

agents.name: CDC
agents.description: Federal public health agency setting national masking guidelines

agents.name: Joe Biden
agents.description: US President advocating for public health safety measures

agents.name: Ron DeSantis
agents.description: Governor of Florida, prominent opponent of mask mandates

agents.name: Republican Party
agents.description: Political party generally opposing federal mandates

agents.name: Democratic Party
agents.description: Political party generally supporting public health mandates

agents.name: Urban Liberal Cluster
agents.description: High-density population centers with high compliance

agents.name: Rural Conservative Cluster
agents.description: Low-density population centers with low compliance

notes: Pro-side defined as supporting government-mandated mask requirements in public spaces. Against-side defined as opposing such mandates, favoring individual choice. Data quality: Early 2021 data is robust; mid-year data reflects significant volatility due to CDC guidance shifts.
